methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $14M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Aclarity, a Series A climate and energy technology company founded by Julie Bliss Mullen, is being valued within a sector landscape that includes one public comparable (LNZA) and eight private peers, notably Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen. The company has raised $14M to date.

The valuation analysis applies a stage-progression methodology, which is applicable given the Series A context and absence of sufficiently dense peer clustering. Using total capital raised as a proxy base ($14M) and applying a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier for progression from Series A to the next funding round, the implied valuation is $28M. A peer-median approach proved not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparable data within the Climate & Energy sector; while nine same-sector peers exist, none of the narrow-filter categories yielded the three or more datapoints necessary for reliable median calculation.

The triangulated valuation range is $24M–$32M, with a median of $28M. This single-method result carries material uncertainty: the range reflects +15% and –15% sensitivity bands around the median, but rests on one applicable methodology. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the 2.0x step-up multiplier for Aclarity's specific risk profile and market conditions, and the lack of direct peer valuation anchors at comparable stage and capital position. A subsequent funding round or acquisition of additional private comp data would significantly narrow this band.
